allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps scientific-method-evidence-testing understand how parts interact before deciding what evidence would actually resolve the issue.
allies.source_quote: SMET builds systemic expertise. It shifts focus from static details to patterns of change by requiring analysis of how individual parts and procedures interact within an underlying system.

allies.rationale_text: Experimentation gives scientific-method-evidence-testing a practical way to challenge hypotheses instead of debating them abstractly.
allies.source_quote: The Scientific Method Evidence Testing (SMET) model is fundamentally a strategy for understanding the world through a process of experimentation.

allies.rationale_text: Falsifiability keeps scientific-method-evidence-testing honest by requiring disconfirming evidence criteria before the data review starts.
allies.source_quote: Works best when hypotheses can be falsified clearly and the team defines what would count as disconfirming evidence before diving into the data.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias is the psychological enemy of scientific-method-evidence-testing because it quietly converts testing into answer defense.
antagonists.source_quote: scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when disconfirming data is discounted after hypothesis commitment.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with confirmation-bias when disconfirming data is discounted after hypothesis commitment.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scientific-method-evidence-testing vs confirmation-bias: scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when disconfirming data is discounted after hypothesis commitment.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with status-quo-bias when incumbent assumptions are protected from falsification.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scientific-method-evidence-testing vs status-quo-bias: scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when incumbent assumptions are protected from falsification.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with anchoring when over-reliance on the first hypothesis encountered prevents equal evaluation of alternative explanations.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scientific-method-evidence-testing vs anchoring: scientific-method-evidence-testing conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when over-reliance on the first hypothesis encountered prevents equal evaluation of alternative explanations.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on explicit conflict blocks and direct allies in experimentation and systems analysis, so the file stays close to that surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation expansion beyond the explicitly named testing and bias-conflict structure.
open_questions: Critical-thinking is a source-real ally, but experimentation and falsifiability were the cleaner bounded anchors into the current curated substrate.
